step_by_step_thinking:
Your Answer:

I agree with the previous conclusion. Doxycycline is minimally affected by renal function because it is mainly cleared through hepatic metabolism and biliary excretion, unlike amphotericin B, vancomycin, and gentamicin—whose dosing must be adjusted due to significant reliance on renal elimination.
